headline: Is Lockheed Martin (LMT) Still Trading At A Discount After Contract Wins?
summary: Lockheed Martin stock has returned 63.1% over the past five years, and today the valuation checks suggest the market price may still sit below an intrinsic value estimate based on a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) approach. Over 5 years, a 63.1% total return indicates Lockheed Martin has already rewarded patient shareholders, so any valuation upside now matters more for fresh capital. Recent contract wins and acquisition plans can support expectations for future defense cash flows. At the same...

headline: GE Aerospace or Lockheed Martin: Which Aerospace and Defense Stock Is a Better Buy for 2026?
summary: GE Aerospace posts robust margin gains and surging commercial aviation growth, while Lockheed Martin leans on defense contracts and lower valuations.

headline: 3 Resilient Defense Stocks to Buy in July
summary: Defense stocks are the rare corner of the market where geopolitical anxiety, fiscal generosity and multi-year revenue visibility all converge at once. With the Fiscal Year 2027 investment request by the Department of War totaling $756.8 billion, and President Trump declaring that “our Military Budget for the year 2027 should not be $1 Trillion Dollars, ... 3 Resilient Defense Stocks to Buy in July

headline: Lockheed Martin (LMT) Eyes $3.5 Billion Ultra Maritime Deal In Undersea Defense
summary: Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) is reported to be the leading bidder to acquire Ultra Maritime in a deal valued at around US$3.5b. The potential acquisition would expand Lockheed Martin’s presence in undersea warfare and anti-submarine technologies. This news comes at a time of heightened global security tensions and growing demand for advanced undersea defense capabilities. Lockheed Martin is a major defense contractor with a broad portfolio across aerospace, missiles, and defense systems. This...
